summary_en: ¥46/share annual dividend (¥41 regular + ¥5 10th-listing-anniversary special) vs. ¥39 prior year. Regular dividend increased ¥2/share from prior guidance of ¥39. Payout ratio 28.2%, aligning with 25%+ target. Cumulative dividend progression maintained; special component commemorates March 2026 10th anniversary of listing.
title_en: Notice of Full-Year Consolidated Earnings Forecast Variance and Dividend of Surplus Funds (Dividend Increase and 10th Listing Anniversary Dividend)
translation_en_brief: Shoei Yakuhin announced on May 14, 2026, that its board has decided to increase the annual dividend for fiscal year ending March 31, 2026, to ¥46 per share, comprising ¥41 in regular dividend and ¥5 as a 10th listing anniversary special dividend. The regular dividend of ¥41/share represents a ¥2 increase from the prior guidance of ¥39/share issued on May 12, 2025. Total dividend payout is ¥157.5 million, yielding a payout ratio of 28.2%, in line with the company's target of maintaining 25%+ payout ratio. The increase reflects strong earnings performance in FY3/26 (net profit of ¥557 million vs. guidance of ¥417 million), driven by robust domestic orders in the chemical business and successful cost pass-through amid rising raw material prices. The company continues a progressive dividend policy based on prior-year per-share amounts while securing internal reserves for future operations. The dividend record date is March 31, 2026, with payment effective June 26, 2026, subject to shareholder approval at the June 25, 2026 ordinary general meeting.
